behaviors.description: The speech-to-text output preserves speaker labels so that the summary can attribute statements to specific attendees.
behaviors.justification: Failure depends on real speech-model diarization output (speaker labeling quality), which a stub would hide. This is not just wrapper logic. Rejected Workflow: behavior text targets preservation of labels at STT/handoff quality, not validating a full multi-agent pipeline outcome across multiple model decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Use a real multi-speaker meeting audio clip against the real STT API; assert speaker-labeled utterances are present and preserved into the summary-input payload format consumed downstream.

behaviors.description: Transcript parsing extracts explicit task assignments (assignee + task text) into the summary input structure.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is deterministic parsing/mapping logic and can fail within a single local boundary using transcript text fixtures. No real OS/network/subprocess or model judgment is required.
behaviors.test_description: Feed representative transcript snippets (including edge phrasing) into the parser function and assert extracted `{assignee, task_text}` entries exactly match expected summary-input structure.

behaviors.description: The live summary output contains at least one action item for every attendee who was assigned a task during the meeting.
behaviors.justification: Correctness depends on real LLM content decisions (coverage/completeness by attendee). Deterministic stubs can always return compliant output and miss true failures.
behaviors.test_description: Submit a real transcript with known per-attendee assignments to the live summarization call and assert each assigned attendee appears with at least one action item in output.

behaviors.description: Decision extraction logic routes identified decisions into the `decisions` section schema rather than generic narrative fields.
behaviors.justification: Behavior text is routing logic after decisions are already identified. This is local schema-mapping behavior; no real infra or model-output quality is needed.
behaviors.test_description: Pass pre-identified decision objects through the routing/mapping function and assert they land only in `decisions` schema fields, not narrative fields.

behaviors.description: Key decisions mentioned in the transcript appear in the live summary's `decisions` section, not buried in the narrative.
behaviors.justification: This checks model extraction and placement quality from raw transcript content. Mocked model responses would mask failures in real decision detection/placement.
behaviors.test_description: Run real transcript samples containing explicit decisions through live summarization and assert those decisions are represented in `decisions` section rather than only narrative text.

behaviors.description: When the audio file is longer than 60 minutes, the service splits it into segments and processes them sequentially, merging the results into a single coherent summary.
behaviors.justification: Requires multiple sequential model-involved steps (segment transcribe/summarize/merge) where failures emerge from cross-step handoff and accumulation. Single-step Agentic tests can miss merge/coherence regressions.
behaviors.test_description: Run a real >60-minute meeting recording through production segmentation flow; verify segment order handling, merged structured output integrity, and cross-segment coherence in final summary.

behaviors.description: The full pipeline (upload audio, transcribe, summarize, deliver structured output) completes without manual intervention for a real meeting recording.
behaviors.justification: This is explicit end-to-end multi-stage execution with real AI steps and real delivery path. Real failures appear only across chained stages; lower levels cannot validate no-manual-intervention completion.
behaviors.test_description: Execute an end-to-end run with a real meeting recording in production-like environment: upload, STT, summarization, and structured output delivery; assert terminal success and schema-valid result without human intervention.
